query: Does giving an LLM a structured negotiation script improve a human's bargaining outcomes?

passages.text: In a randomized study of 240 participants, users coached by an LLM that followed a structured BATNA-anchoring script reached agreements with 12% higher joint surplus than an unscripted-chatbot control, and the effect held after controlling for prior negotiation experience.

Pairing novice negotiators with a turn-by-turn LLM advisor that prompted them to state their reservation value before conceding raised their individual payoff by 0.4 standard deviations relative to negotiating alone.
Participants reported higher confidence when an AI assistant suggested concession sequences, though the paper notes subjective confidence did not always track objective deal quality.
A simulation of automated agent-to-agent bargaining showed that explicit scripts reduced impasse rates, but the agents were not human and no human-outcome measure was reported.
Large language models can be prompted to role-play distinct negotiation personas, which is useful for generating training dialogues.
Transformer attention complexity grows quadratically with sequence length, motivating sparse-attention variants for long documents.
Retrieval-augmented generation grounds model outputs in an external corpus to reduce hallucination on factual queries.

query: What sample sizes are typical in studies measuring AI coaching effects on negotiation skill?

passages.text: Across eight published trials of AI negotiation coaching, sample sizes ranged from 48 to 612 participants, with a median of 180; only three were powered to detect effects smaller than 0.3 standard deviations.

The authors recruited 312 undergraduates and assigned them to AI-coached, human-coached, or no-coaching arms, reporting that the AI arm needed roughly 100 participants per cell to detect the observed effect at 80% power.
A meta-analysis of computer-assisted negotiation training emphasized that underpowered samples (n < 60) dominated early work and likely inflated reported effect sizes.
Effect sizes in behavioral negotiation research are commonly reported as Cohen's d, which complicates pooling when studies differ in outcome scaling.
Recruiting professional negotiators is expensive, so most studies rely on student convenience samples.
Voyage embeddings produce 1024-dimensional vectors suitable for cosine-similarity retrieval over academic text.
BM25 is a lexical ranking function that scores documents by term frequency and inverse document frequency.

query: Do LLM negotiation agents concede too quickly compared to humans?

passages.text: When pitted against human counterparts, GPT-class negotiation agents conceded on price an average of 1.8 rounds earlier than humans and captured 9% less of the surplus, a gap the authors attribute to the model's bias toward agreeableness.
Ablating the politeness-tuned reward made the agent hold firm longer and improved its captured value, directly linking premature concession to alignment tuning.
Human negotiators in the same protocol anchored aggressively and rarely made the first concession, whereas the baseline model frequently opened with a near-final offer.
The study used an ultimatum-game variant with monetary stakes paid as a bonus.
Concession behavior was coded from transcripts by two raters with substantial inter-rater agreement.
Fine-tuning on domain text generally improves retrieval relevance for specialized corpora.
pgvector adds an approximate nearest-neighbor index to Postgres via the HNSW method.

query: How is the quality of an AI-generated research hypothesis evaluated for novelty?

passages.text: The authors operationalized novelty as the cosine distance between a generated hypothesis embedding and its nearest neighbor in an existing literature corpus, avoiding the unreliability of asking the model to rate its own originality.
Hypotheses were scored by expert reviewers on novelty and plausibility, and the embedding-distance proxy correlated r=0.61 with reviewer novelty judgments.
Self-assessed novelty from the language model was systematically inflated, rating nearly every hypothesis as highly original regardless of overlap with prior work.
Grounding each hypothesis in cited contradictions from the source set made its provenance auditable against the underlying papers.
Generated hypotheses were de-duplicated before scoring to avoid rewarding repetition.
SMTP over TLS on port 587 is the standard submission path for outbound email.
Cohen's kappa corrects observed agreement for the agreement expected by chance.

query: Does hybrid lexical-plus-dense retrieval beat dense-only retrieval for finding contradicting scientific claims?

passages.text: Adding a BM25 pass to dense retrieval raised recall of genuinely contradicting claim pairs from 0.71 to 0.88, because lexically overlapping but semantically reframed claims fell below the dense similarity threshold.
The union of dense and lexical candidate pairs surfaced contradictions that shared rare tokens such as dataset and metric names which the embedding model mapped far apart in vector space.
Dense-only retrieval favored paraphrase similarity and missed pairs that disagreed using different terminology for the same construct.
Each candidate pair was tagged with the retriever that surfaced it so per-retriever coverage could be measured.
Reranking the merged candidate list further trimmed false positives before the expensive judgment stage.
Fernet provides symmetric authenticated encryption built on AES-128 in CBC mode with an HMAC.
React escapes interpolated text by default, mitigating a class of cross-site scripting bugs.
